title: Plan of Action and Milestones Open Items Past Due

app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) or CSV lookup updated by GRC
spl:
| inputlookup poam_open.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where due_epoch < now() AND status!="Closed"
| table poam_id, control, owner, due_date, status, risk_rating
| sort due_date

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) or CSV lookup updated by GRC.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `inputlookup poam_open.csv`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Automate CSV refresh from authoritative GRC; (2) RBAC the lookup; (3) weekly leadership digest; (4) integrate CAP tracking.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| inputlookup poam_open.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where due_epoch < now() AND status!="Closed"
| table poam_id, control, owner, due_date, status, risk_rating
| sort due_date
```

Understanding this SPL

**Plan of Action and Milestones Open Items Past Due (CA-5)** — Lists overdue POA&M items for CA-5 plans of action and milestones.

Documented **Data sources**: `inputlookup poam_open.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) or CSV lookup updated by GRC. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Loads rows via `inputlookup` (KV store or CSV lookup) for enrichment or reporting.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due_epoch** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where due_epoch < now() AND status!="Closed"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Plan of Action and Milestones Open Items Past Due (CA-5)**): table poam_id, control, owner, due_date, status, risk_rating
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (overdue POA&M), Single value (count), Timeline
